generatedSentence: A wrinkled former deckhand, now a raconteur known as Old Silas, spent his days editing old maritime logs. His meager income came from the quiet work, a stark retreat from his youth spent on ketches across various latitudes. He lived in a small, windowed room, the insulation barely holding against the chill. A vintage transistor radio, a true relic, always played softly, its buzz a constant companion. He often pondered the kismet that led him from the high seas to this quiet life. His most cherished possession, a small, holstered compass, was always tucked away, a lifesaver from a forgotten storm. He knew, with a quiet knowing, that his past, like an old internet newsgroup, contained stories far greater than his present. He sometimes felt the inward pull of his old life, an unadjusted longing. He'd once been a hero to others, but those days were gone, buried under layers of memory. Suddenly, a blaring beacon from his old truck shattered the peace. A desperate message, encrypted like an ancient tapes recording, flashed across his rudimentary mockup terminal: the oligarchy was attempting to unearth a colossal weapon, a warhead fueled by dangerous radiation. This wasn't just vandalism; it was a global challenge. He felt his old psyche ignite. The message described a facility guarded by infamous janitors, a bizarre cover for their true role. Silas, despite his age, felt the familiar fire of a volunteer infantryman. He saw a motorcycle parked outside, its wheels glinting. He knew this was no ordinary contract; it was a mission to save the world, not some pickled dispute. The urgency was unleashed, his old life unleashing itself once more, demanding an equivalent effort to his youth. Blimey, he felt a primal need to act, to escape this quiet jail. Silas, now clad in a waistcoat and with a rechargeable endoscope holstered into a specialized pocket, trekked through the facility’s lattice-like corridors. The air was thick with the scent of hemlock and the sticky residue of a strange, oobleck-like substance. He moved with a practiced elegance, his movements precise to the centimeter. He found the core chamber, a window revealing the glowing device. A massive buzzsaw, designed by an unknown designer, was already licking at the protective casing. He saw the gleam of platinum in the machinery, an indicator of the vast accrued wealth financing this horror. He had to stop it. He saw the facility’s visionaries, true believers in their twisted doctrine, kneeling before the activation panel. He bucked against his age, his determination a prickly urgency. This was his final act of defiance, possibly the last. He spotted an artificial control panel, its complex anatomy a testament to evil. He felt a deep, ancestral ingrain push him forward. With a final desperate push, Silas bunted the activation lever, causing the polymorphic energy to destabilize. The facility began to crumble, bricks collapsing like a poorly constructed drydock. He made his escape, feeling the sting of overtime and the bittersweet taste of victory. His efforts had been validated, a quiet prize for his years of quiet work. He thought of his quiet victoria against the wanted destruction. He knew a forced relocation and recruitment of new personnel would follow for the oligarchy. He sipped on some cool lemonade, a simple pleasure after the ordeal, ignoring the sounds of a nearby pipe pissing water. He saw a quirky ostrich mural painted on a nearby wall, a symbol of absurd freedom, beside a strange koala statue. The world was safe, for now. He was just an old man who had backed the right side. The mandarine sunset painted the sky, sweet as honey, a testament to the world he had saved from grunge. His lungs filled with clean air, a grape-like flavor on the breeze. He had made a difference, proving his inimitable spirit, a rondel of courage. The taste of kumquat in his drink was surprisingly refreshing.

rawPanelSentences: A **wrinkled** former **deckhand**, now a **raconteur** known as Old Silas, spent his days **editing** old maritime logs. His meager **income** came from the quiet work, a stark **retreat** from his youth spent on **ketches** across various **latitudes**. He lived in a small, **windowed** **room**, the **insulation** barely holding against the chill. A vintage **transistor** radio, a true **relic**, always played softly, its buzz a constant companion. He often pondered the **kismet** that led him from the high seas to this quiet life. His most cherished possession, a small, **holstered** compass, was always **tucked** away, a **lifesaver** from a forgotten storm. He knew, with a quiet **knowing**, that his past, like an old **internet** **newsgroup**, contained stories far **greater** than his present. He sometimes felt the **inward** pull of his old life, an **unadjusted** longing. He'd once been a **hero** to **others**, but those days were gone, buried under **layers** of memory.

Suddenly, a blaring **beacon** from his old **truck** shattered the peace. A desperate message, encrypted like an ancient **tapes** recording, flashed across his rudimentary **mockup** terminal: the **oligarchy** was attempting to **unearth** a **colossal** weapon, a **warhead** fueled by dangerous **radiation**. This wasn't just **vandalism**; it was a global **challenge**. He felt his old **psyche** ignite. The message described a facility guarded by **infamous** **janitors**, a bizarre cover for their true role. Silas, despite his age, felt the familiar fire of a **volunteer** **infantry**man. He saw a **motorcycle** parked outside, its **wheels** glinting. He knew this was no ordinary **contract**; it was a mission to save the world, not some **pickled** dispute. The urgency was **unleashed**, his old life **unleash**ing itself once more, demanding an **equivalent** effort to his youth. **Blimey**, he felt a primal need to act, to escape this quiet **jail**.

Silas, now clad in a **waistcoat** and with a **rechargeable** **endoscope** **holstered** into a specialized pocket, **trekked** through the facility’s **lattice**-like corridors. The air was thick with the scent of **hemlock** and the **sticky** residue of a strange, **oobleck**-like substance. He moved with a practiced **elegance**, his movements precise to the **centimeter**. He found the core chamber, a **window** revealing the glowing device. A massive **buzzsaw**, designed by an unknown **designer**, was already **licking** at the protective casing. He saw the gleam of **platinum** in the machinery, an indicator of the vast **accrued** wealth financing this horror. He had to stop it. He saw the facility’s **visionaries**, true believers in their twisted doctrine, **kneeling** before the activation panel. He **bucked** against his age, his determination a **prickly** urgency. This was his final act of defiance, **possibly** the last. He spotted an **artificial** control panel, its complex **anatomy** a testament to evil. He felt a deep, ancestral **ingrain** push him forward.

With a final desperate push, Silas **bunted** the activation lever, causing the **polymorph**ic energy to destabilize. The facility began to crumble, **bricks** collapsing like a poorly constructed **drydock**. He made his escape, feeling the sting of **overtime** and the bittersweet taste of victory. His efforts had been **validated**, a quiet **prize** for his years of quiet work. He thought of his quiet **victoria** against the **wanted** destruction. He knew a forced **relocation** and **recruitment** of new personnel would follow for the oligarchy. He sipped on some cool **lemonade**, a simple pleasure after the ordeal, ignoring the sounds of a nearby pipe **pissing** water. He saw a quirky **ostrich** mural painted on a nearby wall, a symbol of absurd freedom, beside a strange **koala** statue. The world was safe, for now. He was just an old man who had **backed** the right side. The **mandarine** sunset painted the sky, sweet as **honey**, a testament to the world he had saved from **grunge**. His **lung**s filled with clean air, a **grape**-like flavor on the breeze. He had made a difference, proving his **inimitable** spirit, a **rondel** of courage. The taste of **kumquat** in his drink was surprisingly refreshing.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, noir comic art style, thick white gutters, clean black borders. An older man with a weathered face, deep-set eyes, graying temples, and a short, neatly trimmed beard. TOP-LEFT PANEL: Silas, a grizzled man with a graying beard, meticulously hand-editing old documents at a cluttered desk in a dimly lit, cozy room, medium shot, slightly from above. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: Silas, a mature man with a determined expression, looking intently at a flickering, urgent message on a primitive computer screen, with a powerful motorcycle visible through a window behind him, close-up, dramatic low angle. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: Silas, an older man with wrinkles around his eyes, stealthily navigating a dark, metallic industrial corridor, a massive, active buzzsaw visible in the background, near figures in ceremonial robes kneeling before a console, wide shot, atmospheric. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: Silas, a man with a look of quiet triumph and relief, walking away from a crumbling, smoking industrial facility against a vibrant mandarine sunset, holding a glass of lemonade, long shot, epic. cel-shading, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting. No text, no speech bubbles, no dialogue, no children.

definitions.definition: Money one earns by working or by capitalising on the work of others.
Money coming in to a fund, account, or policy.
A coming in; arrival; entrance; introduction.
A newcomer or arrival; an incomer.
An entrance-fee.
A coming in as by influx or inspiration, hence, an inspired quality or characteristic, as courage or zeal; an inflowing principle.
A disease or ailment without known or apparent cause, as distinguished from one induced by accident or contagion; an oncome.
That which is taken into the body as food; the ingesta; sometimes restricted to the nutritive, or digestible, portion of the food.
A small wheel or roller, specifically the wheel of a gun carriage.
The ball on top of a flagpole.

On a wooden mast, a circular disc (or sometimes a rectangle) of wood near or at the top of the mast, usually with holes or sheaves to reeve signal halyards; also a temporary or emergency place for a lookout. "Main" refers to the mainmast, whereas a truck on another mast may be called (on the mizzenmast, for example) "mizzen-truck".
A semi-tractor ("semi") trailer; a lorry.
definitions.example: Mexican open-bed trucks haul most of the fresh produce that comes into the United States from Mexico.

definitions.definition: Any motor vehicle designed for carrying cargo, including delivery vans, pickups, and other motorized vehicles (including passenger autos) fitted with a bed designed to carry goods.
A garden cart, a two-wheeled wheelbarrow.
A small wagon or cart, of various designs, pushed or pulled by hand or pulled by an animal, as with those in hotels for moving luggage, or in libraries for transporting books.
A pantechnicon (removal van).
A flatbed railway car; a flatcar.

A pivoting frame, one attached to the bottom of the bed of a railway car at each end, that rests on the axle and which swivels to allow the axle (at each end of which is a solid wheel) to turn with curves in the track; a bogie.
The part of a skateboard or roller skate that joins the wheels to the deck, consisting of a hanger, baseplate, kingpin, and bushings, and sometimes mounted with a riser in between.
A platform with wheels or casters.
Dirt or other messiness.
To drive a truck: Generally a truck driver's slang.
To convey by truck.
definitions.example: Last week, Cletus trucked 100 pounds of lumber up to Dubuque.

definitions.definition: To travel or live contentedly.
definitions.example: Keep on trucking!

definitions.definition: To persist, to endure.
definitions.example: Keep on trucking!

definitions.definition: (film production) To move a camera parallel to the movement of the subject.
To fight or otherwise physically engage with.
To run over or through a tackler in American football.
To pull or gather up (an item of fabric).
To push into a snug position; to place somewhere safe or somewhat hidden.
definitions.example: Tuck in your shirt.  I tucked in the sheet.  He tucked the $10 bill into his shirt pocket.

definitions.definition: (often with "in" or "into") To eat; to consume.
To fit neatly.
definitions.example: The sofa tucks nicely into that corner.  Kenwood House is tucked into a corner of Hampstead Heath.

definitions.definition: To curl into a ball; to fold up and hold one's legs.
definitions.example: The diver tucked, flipped, and opened up at the last moment.

definitions.definition: To sew folds; to make a tuck or tucks in.
definitions.example: to tuck a dress

definitions.definition: To full, as cloth.
(of a drag queen, trans woman, etc.) To conceal one’s penis and testicles, as with a gaff or by fastening them down with adhesive tape.
definitions.example: Honey, have you tucked today? We don’t wanna see anything nasty down there.

definitions.definition: (when playing scales on piano keys) To keep the thumb in position while moving the rest of the hand over it to continue playing keys that are outside the thumb.
A biological organ of vertebrates that controls breathing and oxygenates the blood.
(plural) Capacity for exercise or exertion; breath.
definitions.example: He hasn't the lungs to play long rallies like he use to.

definitions.definition: That which supplies oxygen or fresh air, such as trees, parklands, forest, etc., to a place.
Someone or something that saves lives.
definitions.example: My dad loves his job as a surf lifesaver.

definitions.definition: Someone or something that is very useful or helpful.
definitions.example: Thank you so much for fixing my car; you're a real lifesaver!

definitions.definition: A flavoured beverage consisting of water, lemon, and sweetener, sometimes ice, served mainly as a refreshment.
A clear, usually carbonated, beverage made from lemon or artificial lemon flavouring, water, and sugar.
Recreational drugs of poor or weak quality, especially heroin.
To change a text, or a document.
To be the editor of a publication.
definitions.example: He edits the Chronicle.

definitions.definition: To change the contents of a file, website, etc.
definitions.example: Wikipedia is an interactive encyclopedia which allows anybody to edit and improve articles.

definitions.definition: To alter the DNA sequence of a chromosome; to perform gene splicing.
To alter a film by cutting and splicing frames.
To cut short or otherwise alter an improvised scene.
To lend itself to editing in a certain way.
An act or instance of something being edited.
A circular device capable of rotating on its axis, facilitating movement or transportation or performing labour in machines.
The breaking wheel, an old instrument of torture.
A person with a great deal of power or influence; a big wheel.
The lowest straight in poker: ace, 2, 3, 4, 5.
A wheelrim.
A round portion of cheese.
A Catherine wheel firework.
A rolling or revolving body; anything of a circular form; a disk; an orb.
A turn or revolution; rotation; compass.
A recurring or cyclical course of events.
definitions.example: the wheel of life

definitions.definition: A dollar.
A crown coin; a "cartwheel".
To roll along on wheels.
definitions.example: Wheel that trolley over here, would you?

definitions.definition: To transport something or someone using any wheeled mechanism, such as a wheelchair.
To ride a bicycle or tricycle.
To change direction quickly, turn, pivot, whirl, wheel around.
To cause to change direction quickly, turn.
To travel around in large circles, particularly in the air.
definitions.example: The vulture wheeled above us.

definitions.definition: To put into a rotatory motion; to cause to turn or revolve; to make or perform in a circle.
(by meronymy) An automobile or other vehicle; a set of wheels
Well-developed thigh muscles.
To perceive the truth or factuality of; to be certain of or that.
definitions.example: He knew something terrible was going to happen.

definitions.definition: To be aware of; to be cognizant of.
definitions.example: Did you know Michelle and Jack were getting divorced? ― Yes, I knew.

definitions.definition: To be acquainted or familiar with; to have encountered.
definitions.example: I know your mother, but I’ve never met your father.

definitions.definition: To experience.
definitions.example: Their relationship knew ups and downs.

definitions.definition: To be able to distinguish, to discern, particularly by contrast or comparison; to recognize the nature of.
definitions.example: I wouldn't know one from the other.

definitions.definition: To recognize as the same (as someone or something previously encountered) after an absence or change.
To understand or have a grasp of through experience or study.
definitions.example: His mother tongue is Italian, but he also knows French and English.

definitions.definition: To have sexual relations with. This meaning normally specified in modern English as e.g. to ’know someone in the biblical sense’ or to ‘know Biblically.’
To have knowledge; to have information, be informed.
definitions.example: He knows about 19th century politics.

definitions.definition: To be or become aware or cognizant.
definitions.example: Did you know Michelle and Jack were getting divorced? ― Yes, I knew.

definitions.definition: To be acquainted (with another person).
To be able to play or perform (a song or other piece of music).
definitions.example: Do you know "Blueberry Hill"?

definitions.definition: The act or condition of having knowledge.
Possessing knowledge or understanding; intelligent.
Shrewd or showing clever awareness.
definitions.example: a knowing rascal

definitions.definition: Suggestive of private knowledge.
Deliberate
A kind of low four-wheeled pleasure carriage, with a calash top, designed for two persons and the driver who occupies a high seat in front.
To render valid.
To check or prove the validity of; verify.
To have its validity successfully proven.
definitions.example: The data file cannot be imported because it doesn't validate.

definitions.definition: Declared or made valid.
Having experienced emotional validation.
The shooting forth of anything from a point or surface, like diverging rays of light.
definitions.example: heat radiation

definitions.definition: The process of radiating waves or particles.
The transfer of energy via radiation (as opposed to convection or conduction).
Radioactive energy.
That which remains; that which is left after loss or decay; a remaining portion.
Something old and outdated, possibly kept for sentimental reasons.
A part of the body of a saint, or an ancient religious object, kept for veneration.
(often of guitars) To cause (an object) to appear old or worn, to distress.
A circular saw.
To cut with a circular saw.
To spin rapidly like the blades of a circular saw.
To produce a loud, harsh noise like that of a circular saw.
To put something in a holster.
The act of insulating; detachment from other objects; isolation.
The state of being insulated; detachment from other objects; isolation.
Any of a variety of materials designed to reduce the flow of heat, either from or into a building.
The act of separating a body from others by nonconductors, so as to prevent the transfer of electricity, heat, or sound
The state of a body so separated.
A medium in which it is possible to maintain an electrical field with little supply of energy from additional sources.
Any set of computer networks that communicate using the Internet Protocol. (An intranet.)
The Internet, the largest global internet.
An internet connection, internet connectivity, access to the internet.
definitions.example: Do you have internet at your place?  My internet is down and I want to check my email.

definitions.definition: A fictitious unit of scoring, awarded for making outstanding posts.
To use the Internet; to search for something using the Internet; to surf the Internet.
definitions.example: Having no idea what that means, I am internetting like mad.

definitions.definition: (semiconductors) A solid-state semiconductor device, with three terminals, which can be used for amplification, switching, voltage stabilization, signal modulation, and many other functions.
A transistor radio.
A single thickness of some material covering a surface.
definitions.example: After the first coat of paint dried, he applied another layer.

definitions.definition: A (usually) horizontal deposit; a stratum.
definitions.example: I find seven-layer cake a bit too rich.

definitions.definition: One of the items in a hierarchy.
definitions.example: mired in layers of deceit

definitions.definition: (by analogy to a stack of transparencies) one in a stack of (initially transparent) drawing surfaces that comprise an image; used to keep elements of an image separate so that they can be modified independently from one another.
To cut or divide (something) into layers
To arrange (something) in layers.
definitions.example: Layer the ribbons on top of one another to make an attractive pattern.

definitions.definition: Grace, refinement, and beauty in movement, appearance, or manners.
definitions.example: The bride was elegance personified.

definitions.definition: Restraint and grace of style.
definitions.example: The simple dress had a quiet elegance.

definitions.definition: The beauty of an idea characterized by minimalism and intuitiveness while preserving exactness and precision.
definitions.example: The proof of the theorem had a pleasing elegance.

definitions.definition: A refinement or luxury.
An SI unit of length equal to 10-2 metres. Symbol: cm
Someone who looks after the maintenance and cleaning of a public building.
A doorman.
Willful damage to or destruction of any property, such as graffiti or defacement.
definitions.example: As we turned down the street I was appalled by the vandalism: spray paint and smashed windows that were everywhere.

definitions.definition: The act of pulling back or withdrawing, as from something dangerous, or unpleasant.
The act of reversing direction and receding from a forward position.
A peaceful, quiet place affording privacy or security.
A peaceful, quiet place in which to urinate and defecate: an outhouse; a lavatory.
A period of retirement, seclusion, or solitude.
A period of meditation, prayer or study.
Withdrawal by military force from a dangerous position or from enemy attack.
A signal for a military withdrawal.
A bugle call or drumbeat signaling the lowering of the flag at sunset, as on a military base.
A military ceremony to lower the flag.
The move of a piece from a threatened position.
To withdraw from a position, go back.
(of a glacier) To shrink back due to generally warmer temperatures.
To slope back.
definitions.example: a retreating forehead

definitions.definition: A mixture of cornstarch and water with unusual physical properties.
A large flightless bird (Struthio camelus) native to Africa.
One who buries one's head in the sand instead of acknowledging problems
Somebody who possesses great bravery and carries out extraordinary or noble deeds.
A role model.
The protagonist in a work of fiction.
The current player, especially an hypothetical player for example and didactic purposes. Compare: villain. Not to be confused with hero call.
definitions.example: Let's discuss how to play if the hero has KK, and there's an ace on board.

definitions.definition: A large sandwich made from meats and cheeses; a hero sandwich.
(food styling) The product chosen from several candidates to be photographed.
The eye-catching top portion of a web page, sometimes including a hero image; the portion above the fold.
To free from a leash, or as from a leash.
definitions.example: He unleashed his dog in the park.

definitions.definition: To let go; to release.
definitions.example: He unleashed his fury.

definitions.definition: To precipitate; to bring about.
Not leashed; without a leash on it.
Freed from any restraint, physical or otherwise.
definitions.example: unleashed potential

definitions.definition: Relatively large in scale, size, extent, number (i.e. having many parts or members) or duration (i.e. relatively long); very big.
definitions.example: A great storm is approaching our shores.

definitions.definition: Of larger size or more importance than others of its kind.
definitions.example: the great auk

definitions.definition: (qualifying nouns of family relationship) Involving more generations than the qualified word implies — as many extra generations as repetitions of the word great (from 1510s). [see Derived terms]
definitions.example: great-grandfather, great-great-grandfather, great-great-great-grandfather

definitions.definition: (followed by 'with') Pregnant; large with young; full of.
definitions.example: great with child

definitions.definition: (except with 'friend' and similar words such as 'mate','buddy') Intimate; familiar.
Extreme or more than usual.
definitions.example: great worry

definitions.definition: Of significant importance or consequence; important.
definitions.example: a great decision

definitions.definition: (applied to actions, thoughts and feelings) Arising from or possessing idealism; admirable; superior; commanding; heroic; illustrious; eminent.
definitions.example: a great deed

definitions.definition: Impressive or striking.
definitions.example: a great show of wealth

definitions.definition: Much in use; favoured.
definitions.example: Poetry was a great convention of the Romantic era.

definitions.definition: (applied to persons) Endowed with extraordinary powers; of exceptional talents or achievements; uncommonly gifted; able to accomplish vast results; remarkable; strong; powerful; mighty; noble.
definitions.example: a great hero, scholar, genius, philosopher, writer etc.

definitions.definition: Title referring to an important leader.
definitions.example: Alexander the Great

definitions.definition: Doing or exemplifying (a characteristic or pursuit) on a large scale; active or enthusiastic.
definitions.example: He's not a great one for reading.

definitions.definition: (often followed by 'at') Skilful or adroit.
definitions.example: You are great at singing.

definitions.definition: Very good; excellent; wonderful; fantastic (from 1848).
definitions.example: Dinner was great.

definitions.definition: Intensifying a word or expression, used in mild oaths.
definitions.example: Great Scott!

definitions.definition: To make a slow or arduous journey.
To journey on foot, especially to hike through mountainous areas.
To travel by ox wagon.
Extremely large or on a great scale.
definitions.example: A single puppy can make a colossal mess.

definitions.definition: A place or institution for the confinement of persons held in lawful custody or detention, especially for minor offenses or with reference to some future judicial proceeding.
Confinement in a jail.
The condition created by the requirement that a horse claimed in a claiming race not be run at another track for some period of time (usually 30 days).
In dodgeball and related games, the area where players who have been struck by the ball are confined.
(FreeBSD) A kind of sandbox for running a guest operating system instance.
To imprison.
A tree-dwelling marsupial, Phascolarctos cinereus, that resembles a small bear with a broad head, large ears and sharp claws, mainly found in eastern Australia.
A small, orange citrus-like fruit which is native to Asia (Citrus japonica, syn. Fortunella japonica).
A fore-and-aft rigged sailing vessel with two masts, main and mizzen, the mizzen being stepped forward of the rudder post.
A hangman.
To free from a leash, or as from a leash.
definitions.example: He unleashed his dog in the park.

definitions.definition: To let go; to release.
definitions.example: He unleashed his fury.

definitions.definition: To precipitate; to bring about.
The angular distance north or south from a planet's equator, measured along the meridian of that particular point.
An imaginary line (in fact a circumference) around a planet running parallel to the planet's equator.
The relative freedom from restrictions; scope to do something.
definitions.example: His parents gave him a great deal of latitude.

definitions.definition: The angular distance of a heavenly body from the ecliptic.
The extent to which a light-sensitive material can be over- or underexposed and still achieve an acceptable result.
Extent or scope; e.g. breadth, width or amplitude.

definitions.definition: Occupying only a part of the screen (in a window.)

definitions.definition: To desire (to experience desire); to wish.

definitions.definition: (usually second person, often future tense) To be advised to do something (compare should, ought).

definitions.definition: (now colloquial) To lack and be in need of or require (something, such as a noun or verbal noun).

definitions.definition: (now rare) To have occasion for (something requisite or useful); to require or need.
To be lacking or deficient or absent.

definitions.definition: To be in a state of destitution; to be needy; to lack.

Wished for; desired; sought
Subject to immediate detainment by law enforcement authorities on sight.
Used to express anger, excitement, surprise, etc.

Nectar.
Something sweet or desirable.

Of a pale yellow to brownish-yellow colour, like most types of honey.

definitions.definition: Of two sets, having a one-to-one correspondence; equinumerous.

Having the equal ability to combine.

A lever; a pry; also, the hold of a lever.

definitions.definition: (modifying a verb) In the realm of possibility; indicates that the action may successfully be performed (is not impossible) regardless of any facts or circumstances known to, stated by or implied by the speaker that might limit the performance.

definitions.definition: To rest on one's bent knees, sometimes only one; to move to such a position.

In the Bunt, you pushed the nose down, past the vertical and still further, until you were in horizontal inverted flight, and came out on the other side and rolled it out.

Preserved by pickling.

A part of legal studies dealing with laws and jurisdiction related to contracts.

One who enters into military service voluntarily, but who, when in service, is subject to discipline and regulations like other soldiers; -- opposed to conscript; specifically, a voluntary member of the organized militia of a country as distinguished from the standing army.
A person who acts out of his own will without a legal obligation, such as a donor.
A plant that grows spontaneously, without being cultivated on purpose; see volunteer plant in Wikipedia.
A native or resident of the American state of Tennessee.
To enlist oneself as a volunteer.
To do or offer to do something voluntarily.
definitions.example: to volunteer for doing the dishes

definitions.definition: To offer, usually unprompted.
definitions.example: to volunteer an explanation

definitions.definition: To grow without human sowing or intentional cultivation.
To offer the services of (someone else) to do something.
definitions.example: My sister volunteered me to do the dishes.

definitions.definition: A metric form of verse using two rhymes, usually fourteen 8- to 10-syllable lines in three stanzas, with the first lines of the first stanza returning as refrain of the next two.
The verse form rondeau.
A rondelle, (small) circular object.
A long thin medieval dagger with a circular guard and a circular pommel (hence the name).
A small round tower erected at the foot of a bastion.
To go in the reverse direction.
definitions.example: the train backed into the station;  the horse refuses to back

definitions.definition: To support.
definitions.example: I back you all the way;  which horse are you backing in this race?

definitions.definition: (of the wind) To change direction contrary to the normal pattern; that is, to shift anticlockwise in the northern hemisphere, or clockwise in the southern hemisphere.
(of a square sail) To brace the yards so that the wind presses on the front of the sail, to slow the ship.
(of an anchor) To lay out a second, smaller anchor to provide additional holding power.
(of a hunting dog) To stand still behind another dog which has pointed.
To push or force backwards.
definitions.example: The mugger backed her into a corner and demanded her wallet.

definitions.definition: To get upon the back of; to mount.
To place or seat upon the back.
To make a back for; to furnish with a back.
definitions.example: to back books

definitions.definition: To adjoin behind; to be at the back of.
To write upon the back of, possibly as an endorsement.
definitions.example: to back a letter;  to back a note or legal document

definitions.definition: (of a justice of the peace) To sign or endorse (a warrant, issued in another county, to apprehend an offender).
To row backward with (oars).
definitions.example: to back the oars
